eras.title: Origins (1830s–1900)
eras.monologue: I was born in the mud and the ambition of a young city. Before the brick and the steel, I was just a patch of prairie near the river. By the 1880s, the smell of the stockyards wasn't far, but here, I was becoming a hub of industry. The trains rattled my bones daily.
eras.pullQuote: I was born in the mud and the ambition of a young city.
eras.imagePrompt: A sepia-toned archival photograph of a Chicago street in the late 1800s, horse-drawn carriages, brick industrial buildings, Fulton Market area.

eras.title: The Boom (1900–1940)
eras.monologue: The world came to my doorstep. I became a warehouse, a cold storage giant. Men in flat caps hauled crates of poultry and produce through my halls. I was the pantry of the Midwest, bustling with the energy of a thousand merchants.
eras.pullQuote: I was the pantry of the Midwest, bustling with the energy of a thousand merchants.
eras.imagePrompt: A black and white photograph of a busy Chicago market street in the 1920s, vintage trucks, men in flat caps, industrial warehouses.

eras.title: The Turning Point (1940–1970)
eras.monologue: The war changed us. The pace quickened, then slowed as the highways began to carve up the city. I stood firm while the world around me started to shift. The heavy industry remained, but the whispers of change were in the wind.
eras.pullQuote: I stood firm while the world around me started to shift.
eras.imagePrompt: A muted, desaturated photograph of a Chicago industrial district in the 1950s, classic cars, brick warehouses, overcast sky.

eras.title: The Struggle (1970–2000)
eras.monologue: The lights grew dim for a while. The warehouses emptied, and the grit of the city felt heavier. I was a shell of my former self, covered in soot and silence. But even in the quiet, I remembered the noise of the market.
eras.pullQuote: I was a shell of my former self, covered in soot and silence.
eras.imagePrompt: A grainy, vivid photograph of a weathered brick building in a quiet Chicago industrial area, 1980s style, urban decay and resilience.

eras.title: Today & What's Next (2000–present)
eras.monologue: Now, the glass and the tech have arrived. They call me 'Fulton Market' with a new kind of reverence. The smell of expensive coffee has replaced the scent of raw poultry. I am reborn, a witness to a new Chicago rising from the old brick.
eras.pullQuote: I am reborn, a witness to a new Chicago rising from the old brick.
eras.imagePrompt: A crisp, modern photograph of a renovated brick warehouse in Fulton Market, Chicago, trendy restaurants, glass office buildings in the background.
